subject: Create Supabase Realtime Configuration
description: Create supabase/migrations/20240101000008_realtime_config.sql. Enable realtime on active_timers table: ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE active_timers; Ensure RLS policies apply to realtime (they do by default in Supabase). Document in comments that realtime subscriptions are filtered by RLS. Create src/lib/realtime.ts with helper function to create typed channel subscription for active_timers with proper TypeScript types for payload (old_record, new_record, eventType). Include reconnection logic and error handling.
result_summary:
Added Supabase realtime support for active timers in the shared workspace.

What changed:
- Added migration `supabase/migrations/20240101000008_realtime_config.sql` to publish `public.active_timers` to `supabase_realtime`.
- Set `REPLICA IDENTITY FULL` on `public.active_timers` so `user_id`-filtered UPDATE/DELETE subscriptions receive usable old-row data.
- Added `src/lib/realtime.ts` with a typed `createActiveTimerSubscription()` helper, payload normalization/validation, reconnect backoff, status callbacks, unsubscribe cleanup, and injectable client support for testing.
- Updated `src/lib/index.ts` exports.
- Registered the shared realtime helper contract and recorded the REPLICA IDENTITY decision.

Definition-of-done notes:
- Input validation: yes, `userId`/retry config are validated with Zod and incoming realtime payloads are normalized/validated before use.
- Authentication/authorization: no new endpoint; realtime access remains protected by existing Supabase auth + RLS policies.
- Error handling: helper routes invalid payloads and channel failures through `onError` with sanitized messages.
- Tests: `npm run typecheck` passed. Also ran a targeted compiled Node smoke test covering a happy-path payload normalization case and an invalid-payload/error-path subscription case. Full `npm test` is not available because the project currently has no `test` script. The coordinator `run_tests` helper also appears to use the repo parent cwd instead of `/worktracker`.
- No hardcoded secrets introduced.
- Git commit: blocked by sandbox restrictions on `.git/index.lock`, so changes are present in the shared workspace but uncommitted.
